title: dactylorhiza traunsteinerioides subsp. francis-drucei
intro: Dactylorhiza traunsteinerioides subsp. francis-drucei, also known as the Francis Druce's marsh orchid, is a rare species of orchid native to the United Kingdom and Ireland. It is a type of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the Dactylorhiza genus, characterized by its small size and distinctive purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Dactylorhiza traunsteinerioides subsp. francis-drucei have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and tinctures, have been used to treat various health conditions, including colds, bronchitis, and skin conditions, with potential health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function.
